title: The 'this' Keyword 🎯
description: How an object refers specifically to itself without confusion.

slides.title: Who am I? 🪞
Enter `this` 👈

quizData.question: What is the primary function of the 'this' keyword inside a class?

options.text: It refers to the current instance of the object being built or running the code.

quizData.explanation: The 'this' keyword is a self-referential tool used by objects to clarify that they are talking about their OWN properties.

slides.title: Dart's Constructor Shortcut ⚡
slides.content:
## Why we use it

The shortcut parameter we learned earlier `User(this.name)` literally uses the `this` keyword to skip the entire body of the constructor.
It automatically routes the incoming argument directly into the object's property.

In standard Methods, you don't usually need to write `this` unless there's a naming collision, but it's crucial for Constructors!

quizData.question:
Fill in the blank to assign the parameter 'hp' to the object's 'hp' property:
___ .hp = hp;
quizData.explanation: The 'this' keyword points to the current object instance.
